reforms.description: The reform of the family leave model, effective from August 2022, aims to reduce the gender wage gap by providing equal leave days for both parents, encouraging fathers to take a larger share of family leave.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting shared parental responsibilities, the reform is expected to enhance women's participation in the labor market, thereby contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform of the family leave model came into effect in August 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant social issue and has the potential to improve labor market outcomes for women, contributing to overall economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: The reform of the family leave model, which came into effect in August 2022, should help to reduce the gender wage gap.

reforms.description: The government will introduce an R&D tax incentive with an upper limit, which is expected to be insignificant for large companies. This reform aims to stimulate private sector investment in research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages private sector investment in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government will introduce an R&D tax incentive.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of an R&D tax incentive is a significant step towards enhancing private sector R&D investment, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government will introduce an R&D tax incentive with an upper limit, making it insignificant for large companies.

reforms.description: Chronic shortages of study places in higher education institutions are leading to high rejection rates and low educational attainment among young adults, which constrains innovation. The government commits to a credible plan to increase study places in universities and universities of applied sciences.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the number of study places will enhance the supply of skilled graduates, which is essential for driving innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Commit to a credible plan to increase study places in universities and universities of applied sciences.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan addresses a critical bottleneck in the education system that affects the availability of skilled labor necessary for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Commit to a credible plan to increase study places in universities and universities of applied sciences.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has reached a political agreement to increase overall public R&D spending to 1.33% of GDP by 2030, with legislation committing to increase government R&D spending to 1.2% of GDP.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity growth, supporting economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has reached a political agreement to increase public R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhance public R&D funding, which is crucial for revitalizing Finland's innovation ecosystem and addressing productivity challenges.
reforms.source_quote: The government aims to increase Finland’s R&D spending to 4% of GDP by 2030 and will introduce legislation that commits to large and stable government R&D spending.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has established an ambitious goal to boost R&D spending, aiming to enhance the overall innovation ecosystem in the country.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D spending is expected to drive innovation, productivity, and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set an ambitious goal to boost R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing Finland's innovation capacity and competitiveness on a national scale.
reforms.source_quote: The government established an ambitious goal to boost R&D spending.

reforms.description: Finland introduced a new R&D tax credit designed to balance wide accessibility and efficiency, aiming to stimulate private sector investment in research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The R&D tax credit is expected to incentivize firms to invest more in research, thereby enhancing innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new R&D tax credit should balance wide accessibility and efficiency.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the fiscal policy framework to support R&D investment in the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: The new R&D tax credit should balance wide accessibility and efficiency.

reforms.description: The government will introduce a new R&D tax allowance to stimulate stronger business-based R&D spending, addressing the narrow scope of previous incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By broadening the scope of R&D tax incentives, the reform aims to encourage more firms, especially smaller ones, to invest in R&D, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government will introduce a new R&D tax allowance to stimulate stronger business-based R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant shift in the approach to R&D incentives, aiming to enhance business engagement in innovation activities.
reforms.source_quote: The government will also introduce a new R&D tax incentive, which is expected to broaden the scope of firms engaging in business-based R&D.

reforms.description: The government has announced its intention to increase R&D spending to 4% of GDP by 2030, with one third of this amount allocated to public R&D spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is expected to enhance innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced its intention to increase R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding, which is crucial for driving innovation and closing the productivity gap with other Nordic countries.
reforms.source_quote: The government has announced its intention to increase R&D spending to 4% of GDP by 2030, of which one third would be public R&D spending.

reforms.description: The government has announced a scheme to accelerate the immigration of high-skilled workers in certain professions to address skill shortages.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Accelerating the immigration of high-skilled workers is expected to alleviate skill shortages, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced a scheme to accelerate immigration of high-skilled workers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it targets skill shortages that hinder innovation, but it is not as comprehensive as a major overhaul of the immigration system.
reforms.source_quote: It has also announced a scheme to accelerate immigration of high-skilled workers in certain professions.

reforms.description: Finland's Recovery and Resilience Plan includes significant investments in green transition, digitalisation, employment and skills, and healthcare and social services, aimed at supporting post-pandemic economic recovery.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investments are designed to enhance productivity, create jobs, and improve the efficiency of public services, which will support long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Finland has included most of its RRF revenue and expenditure in the budgets for 2021-23.
reforms.importance_rationale: The plan represents a comprehensive approach to addressing multiple sectors critical for economic recovery and future resilience, with substantial funding allocated to transformative projects.
reforms.source_quote: Finland has included most of its RRF revenue and expenditure in the budgets for 2021-23.

reforms.description: The Academy of Finland’s Flagship Programme has been expanded, allocating EUR 25 million to fund new Flagships, with an additional EUR 20 million planned for future years to enhance collaboration between companies and research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases public funding for research initiatives that foster collaboration between academia and industry, enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Flagship Programme has been expanded with additional funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant increase in funding for collaborative research initiatives, which is expected to enhance innovation and research output.
reforms.source_quote: The Academy of Finland’s Flagship Programme has been expanded in the fourth supplementary budget of 2020, allocating EUR 25 million to fund new Flagships.

reforms.description: Business Finland has developed a new model of private-public partnership for long-term R&D and innovation collaboration between firms, research institutes, and universities, with pilot projects beginning in 2020.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates collaboration between public and private sectors, enhancing R&D efforts and innovation outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: Pilot projects began in 2020, indicating the model is in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: Establishes a new framework for collaboration that is expected to enhance innovation and R&D productivity.
reforms.source_quote: Business Finland has developed a new model of private-public partnership for long-term R&D and innovation collaboration.

reforms.description: Finland plans to target the R&D tax credit specifically at SMEs to increase business-based R&D from 2.0% to 3.1% of GDP, helping to achieve a gross domestic R&D expenditure target of 4% of GDP.
reforms.component_name: SME Targeting of R&D Tax Credit
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing R&D investments in SMEs, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is part of the key recommendations for increasing R&D expenditure.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in R&D funding strategy, focusing on SMEs, which are crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Target the R&D tax credit at SMEs. Business-based R&D will increase from 2.0% to 3.1% of GDP.

reforms.description: The Nokia Bridge Programme, implemented between 2011 and 2014, aimed to support the job transition of former Nokia employees, leading to the creation of approximately 400 new companies in Finland.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme facilitated entrepreneurship and innovation by providing support to former employees, thus enhancing the startup ecosystem.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme successfully led to the establishment of new companies.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant structural change in the Finnish innovation landscape by transforming a workforce loss into a source of new entrepreneurial activity.
reforms.source_quote: Nokia’s Bridge Programme in 2011-14...led to the creation of some 400 companies in Finland.

reforms.description: The Finnish government established a National Roadmap for Research, Development and Innovation in April 2020, outlining policy priorities to boost R&D spending and strengthen innovation ecosystems.
reforms.package_name: National Roadmap for Research, Development and Innovation
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The roadmap aims to increase R&D spending, which is essential for enhancing innovation capacity and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The roadmap was put forth in April 2020 and updated in December 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: The roadmap represents a significant policy shift aimed at revitalizing Finland's innovation landscape and achieving ambitious R&D spending targets.
reforms.source_quote: The National Roadmap for Research, Development and Innovation was put forth in April 2020 and updated in December 2021.

reforms.description: Finland's government has committed to increasing public sector R&D spending to 1.33% of GDP by 2030, which is part of a broader strategy to achieve a total R&D spending target of 4% of GDP. This commitment is aimed at ensuring stable innovation support and fostering collaboration with the private sector to boost overall R&D investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D funding is expected to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, thereby enhancing innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Legislation mandating the government to boost its R&D spending is foreseen to enter into force in January 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in Finland's approach to R&D funding, aiming to enhance the overall innovation ecosystem and address historical funding gaps.
reforms.source_quote: In December 2021, Finland reached a political agreement to boost public sector’s R&D spending to 1.33% of GDP by 2030.

reforms.description: The Finnish government plans to introduce a new R&D tax incentive aimed at broadening the R&D base by encouraging a larger number of firms to engage in R&D activities. This incentive will allow for a 50% deduction on R&D-related labor costs and expenditures, with a boosted rate of 150% starting in 2022.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentive is designed to stimulate R&D investment among firms, particularly benefiting start-ups and smaller companies that may lack the resources for significant R&D expenditures.
reforms.status_evidence: The government envisages introducing a tax allowance on 50% of the labour costs and expenditure on services purchased dedicated to R&D activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it introduces a new mechanism for incentivizing R&D investment, which has been lacking in Finland, but it is not as comprehensive as the broader R&D funding reforms.
reforms.source_quote: The new R&D tax incentive will cover more general R&D spending in line with the tax incentives offered by other OECD economies.

reforms.description: The Finnish government is revamping direct R&D support to foster a strong public-private partnership that links basic research with applied research and commercialization of new technologies, particularly targeting small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between research institutions and SMEs is expected to boost innovation and productivity by facilitating the commercialization of new technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is revamping direct R&D support to build a strong public-private partnership.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how R&D support is structured, aiming to enhance collaboration and innovation outcomes across sectors.
reforms.source_quote: Direct R&D support should foster a strong public private partnership in innovation.

reforms.description: Business Finland has launched the Challenge Competitions scheme to provide substantial R&D support to large firms, which is expected to strengthen the innovation ecosystem by encouraging collaboration with SMEs and research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing large firms to subcontract SMEs, the scheme is expected to enhance innovation capacity and competitiveness in the Finnish economy.
reforms.status_evidence: Business Finland launched Challenge Competitions, granting substantial R&D support to large firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance collaboration between large firms and SMEs, with potential positive impacts on innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In 2020 and 2021, Business Finland launched Challenge Competitions, which granted about EUR 180 million of R&D support to large leading firms.

reforms.description: The Finnish government launched the Talent Boost programme to attract international talent, introducing measures to facilitate the immigration of highly skilled foreign workers, including a special residence permit for start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting skilled foreign workers enhances the innovation capacity of the economy and addresses labor shortages in key sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been actively promoting the employment of international students and researchers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Talent Boost programme represents a significant shift in Finland's approach to immigration and labor market integration, aiming to substantially increase the inflow of skilled workers.
reforms.source_quote: Migration policy took a turn in 2017 when the government launched the Talent Boost programme to attract international talent.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has introduced a fast-track decision process for residence permit applications for skilled workers, reducing the processing time to 14 days. This reform aims to attract foreign talent and improve the employment prospects of highly skilled workers in Finland.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting skilled workers, the reform enhances the labor market and contributes to innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has successfully shortened the processing time for residence permits.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the immigration process for skilled workers, which is crucial for enhancing Finland's innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The government recently bolstered its efforts to facilitate the immigration of skilled workers.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has committed EUR 46.2 million from 2021 to 2024 to promote the internationalisation of higher education institutions and education-based immigration, aiming to triple the number of foreign students by 2030.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the number of foreign students enhances the talent pool and contributes to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed funding to promote internationalisation in higher education.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding is a significant step towards enhancing Finland's attractiveness as a destination for foreign students, which is vital for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has set a goal of tripling the number of foreign students enrolling in Finland’s tertiary education from around 5,000 in 2020 to 15,000 by 2030.
